propositions: The async push mode spawns a detached child intended to finish the push after the calling process exits, but no test verifies this parent-exit survival contract.
A new forced-order test should prove the detached push completes after the spawning CLI process has exited.
A test fixture git shim on PATH passes non-push commands through, writes an 'entered push' marker on git push, and blocks on a release file until released.
The test launches rebar create with async push in a subprocess, waits for the marker, and asserts the CLI parent has exited while the push child is still blocked.
After releasing the push, the test polls the bare origin and asserts the created event/commit arrives on the configured remote branch.
The test intentionally does not re-cover spawn-failure logging or PUSH_PENDING surfacing, which are tested elsewhere.
